Home Visit Schedule Student Takeaways
Beyond Academics

Not just a trip.
A new perspective

25 students

T he Seoul Tech & Culture Tour 2026 wasn't just about visiting labs and museums — it was about expanding perspectives, building global networks, and experiencing firsthand how innovation ecosystems thrive at the intersection of technology, culture, and collaboration.

Students returned not only with new knowledge, but with a renewed sense of what's possible in their own academic and professional journeys. What follows are their words — unfiltered, personal, and varied.

Reflections below

Alex Chen

Year 3 · Computer Science

“

The visit to KAIST completely changed how I think about the intersection of hardware and AI. Seeing their semiconductor research facilities made abstract concepts from our courses tangible. Prof. Kate Shim's work on computer vision inspired me to pursue research in this field. The collaborative spirit I witnessed at the CVML lab showed me how impactful teamwork can be in pushing technological boundaries.

Sarah Lim

Year 2 · Computer Science

“

Beyond the tech, understanding Korea's history and experiencing their innovation ecosystem gave me a holistic view of what drives technological advancement. The TOPIS center visit showed me how smart city technology can solve real urban challenges. Meeting students at Yonsei opened my eyes to different academic perspectives and approaches to problem-solving that I never considered before.

Michael Wong

Year 4 · Computer Science

“

Discussing research approaches with Yonsei students showed me how diverse problem-solving can be. We're learning similar concepts but approaching them from different angles. The engineering visit at SKKU exposed me to cutting-edge research methodologies that I'm now incorporating into my final year project. This trip reinforced my desire to pursue graduate studies in AI.

Emily Zhang

Year 3 · Computer Science

“

The Korea University campus tour and discussions with Prof. Paul Seo opened my mind to international research opportunities. Learning about their undergraduate research programs made me realize the importance of starting research early. The cultural immersion, from traditional palaces to modern tech hubs, gave me a deeper appreciation for how culture influences innovation and technological development.

David Lee

Year 2 · Computer Science

“

Visiting the Seoul Gallery at City Hall was eye-opening in understanding how technology and urban planning intersect. The TOPIS briefing demonstrated practical applications of data science and AI in managing city-wide transportation systems. Meeting with Prof. Jeon's students at Yonsei showed me the value of peer collaboration across borders and how different educational systems approach engineering challenges.

Rachel Tan

Year 3 · Computer Science

“

The KAIST AI Hub visit was the highlight of my trip. Seeing the CVML lab's work on machine learning applications in real-world scenarios bridged the gap between theory and practice for me. The collaborative environment and the passion of the researchers inspired me to consider a career in AI research. The group dinner discussions with Korean students enriched my understanding of global tech trends.

Kevin Ng

Year 4 · Computer Science

“

As a final-year student, this tour came at a perfect time. The insights from Prof. Kyuhong Shim at SKKU about industry-academia collaboration shaped my career perspective. Witnessing Korea's innovation ecosystem made me realize the importance of building strong networks and staying curious. The technical depth of the visits combined with cultural exploration created a well-rounded learning experience.

Jessica Liu

Year 2 · Computer Science

“

Meeting Dr. William Stewart at Korea University and learning about their research facilities inspired me to think bigger about my academic goals. The tour showed me that innovation happens at the intersection of different disciplines. The TOPIS center visit particularly interested me as it demonstrated how computer science can directly improve people's daily lives through smart transportation systems.

Ryan Chow

Year 3 · Computer Science

“

The Yonsei Engineering Research Park visit exposed me to world-class research infrastructure and collaborative spaces. Seeing how Korean universities integrate industry partnerships into their research programs gave me ideas for my own academic path. The technical discussions with Prof. Jeon's students challenged my assumptions and broadened my perspective on engineering education globally.

Olivia Chan

Year 2 · Computer Science

“

This tour completely changed my understanding of what's possible in computer science research. The KAIST AI Hub's work on computer vision and machine learning applications was mind-blowing. Meeting Prof. Kate Shim and seeing her lab's achievements motivated me to pursue AI specialization. The cultural experiences in Seoul also taught me the value of work-life balance in a tech-driven society.

Daniel Park

Year 4 · Computer Science

“

As someone considering graduate school, the tour provided invaluable insights into Korean research institutions. SKKU's engineering department showed me innovative approaches to teaching and research that I hadn't seen before. The conversations with professors about their research journeys helped me understand the path from undergraduate to becoming a researcher. The Seoul Gallery visit also reminded me of technology's role in preserving and presenting culture.

Michelle Kwan

Year 3 · Computer Science

“

The Korea University visit was transformative for my academic outlook. Prof. Paul Seo's insights on cross-cultural research collaboration opened new possibilities for my career. Learning about their exchange programs and research opportunities made me seriously consider applying for graduate studies in Korea. The blend of traditional culture and cutting-edge technology throughout Seoul fascinated me.

Andrew Tam

Year 2 · Computer Science

“

The TOPIS center visit was a game-changer for understanding how AI and big data solve real-world urban challenges. Seeing their transportation management system in action made me appreciate the practical applications of what we learn in class. The group discussions with Yonsei students also showed me the importance of international collaboration in tackling global technology challenges.

Sophie Wang

Year 3 · Computer Science

“

What struck me most was the passion and dedication of Korean researchers and students we met. At KAIST's CVML lab, I saw how theoretical research translates into practical innovations. The campus tours at multiple universities helped me understand different academic cultures and research priorities. This experience solidified my decision to pursue a career in AI and machine learning research.

Jason Leung

Year 4 · Computer Science

“

The technical depth of our visits, especially at SKKU and KAIST, exceeded my expectations. Prof. Jongwuk Lee's research on data science applications inspired me to explore new research directions for my thesis. The networking opportunities with Korean students and faculty created connections that I hope to maintain throughout my career. Seoul's tech ecosystem showed me the possibilities of what a city can achieve with the right focus on innovation.

Catherine Ng

Year 2 · Computer Science

“

Experiencing Korea's blend of tradition and innovation gave me a new perspective on how technology shapes society. The Yonsei campus tour and research park visit showed me state-of-the-art facilities and collaborative research environments. Meeting students from different backgrounds and hearing their academic journeys reminded me that success in tech comes from diverse perspectives and continuous learning.

Brian Cheung

Year 3 · Computer Science

“

The tour reinforced my interest in pursuing international research collaborations. KAIST's AI Hub demonstrated world-class research infrastructure and the importance of interdisciplinary approaches. Learning from Prof. Kate Shim about computer vision applications opened my eyes to new research possibilities. The cultural experiences throughout Seoul taught me that innovation thrives where tradition and modernity coexist.

Alicia Ho

Year 2 · Computer Science

“

The Korea University visit was inspiring, especially learning from Dr. William Stewart about their research programs. Seeing how Korean universities foster innovation through industry partnerships gave me ideas about potential career paths. The TOPIS center demonstrated how technology can solve complex urban problems, which aligns perfectly with my interest in smart city applications and public sector technology.

Thomas Yip

Year 4 · Computer Science

“

As a graduating student, this tour provided clarity on my next steps. The SKKU engineering department's approach to research and Prof. Kyuhong Shim's career advice were invaluable. Witnessing Korea's tech ecosystem made me realize the global nature of computer science and the importance of international experience. The technical visits combined with cultural immersion created a perfect balance.

Grace Lau

Year 3 · Computer Science

“

Meeting Prof. Jeon's students at Yonsei and discussing their research projects gave me insights into collaborative learning environments. The engineering research park facilities were impressive and showed me the resources available at top institutions. The Seoul Gallery visit reminded me that technology serves humanity, and we must consider cultural and social impacts in our innovations.

Eric Chan

Year 2 · Computer Science

“

The KAIST AI Hub visit sparked my passion for machine learning research. Prof. Kate Shim's presentation on the CVML lab's work showed me the real-world impact of AI research. The group dinner with Korean students was equally valuable—hearing their experiences and perspectives helped me understand different approaches to learning and innovation. This trip broadened my academic horizons significantly.

Natalie Cheng

Year 3 · Computer Science

“

Korea University's campus and meeting with Prof. Paul Seo made me seriously consider international graduate programs. The research facilities and collaborative culture impressed me deeply. The TOPIS center showed practical applications of big data and AI in urban management, which aligns with my interest in smart cities. This tour helped me see beyond classroom learning to real-world innovation.

Vincent Lai

Year 4 · Computer Science

“

The tour's timing was perfect as I finalize my career plans. Visiting SKKU, KAIST, and Yonsei showed me different research cultures and approaches. The technical discussions with professors and students challenged my thinking and expanded my understanding of computer science research. Seoul's balance of innovation and tradition taught me that successful technology adoption requires cultural sensitivity and understanding.

Hannah Wu

Year 2 · Computer Science

“

What amazed me most was the accessibility of world-class researchers and their willingness to share insights with us. The Yonsei Engineering Research Park visit showed me cutting-edge research spaces and collaborative environments. Meeting students who share my passion for technology but come from different backgrounds enriched my perspective. This experience confirmed my decision to pursue AI specialization.

Steven Choi

Year 3 · Computer Science

“

The Seoul Tech & Culture Tour exceeded all expectations. From SKKU's engineering labs to KAIST's AI Hub, every visit offered unique insights into Korea's innovation ecosystem. The TOPIS center demonstrated how computer science directly improves urban life. Learning from multiple professors about their research journeys and career paths helped me chart my own future in technology and research.
